title: Manual attendance entry
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to manually enter and edit attendance records for employees, so that attendance can be captured even when automated methods are unavailable.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an authorized HR/Company Admin, when I open an employee's attendance for a given date, then I can manually enter or edit punch-in and punch-out times; Given I enter times, when I save, then the system validates that punch-out is after punch-in and flags overlapping or duplicate entries for the same date; Given a manual entry is saved, when the record is persisted, then it is attributed to the entering user with timestamp and made available for attendance review, overtime evaluation, and reporting; Given manual entry is used for an Employee (Non-User) with no self-service login, when I record their attendance, then it is stored against that employee identically to other capture methods; Given manual entry is one of several capture methods, when a record is created manually, then its capture source is recorded as manual for traceability
source: FR 6.18.1 (Attendance Capture Methods — manual entry)

title: Biometric device integration
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want attendance to be captured automatically from integrated biometric devices, so that employee punches are recorded without manual effort.
acceptance_criteria: Given a biometric device is integrated and mapped to employees, when an employee punches in or out, then the attendance record is captured against the correct employee with the exact device timestamp; Given a device transmits records, when they are ingested, then the timestamps are stored, tagged with capture source biometric, and made available for attendance review; Given a punch cannot be matched to a known employee, when ingestion runs, then the record is flagged as unmatched/exception for admin resolution; Given both biometric and other sources record for the same day, when records are consolidated, then duplicates are reconciled without double-counting worked hours
source: FR 6.18.1 (Attendance Capture Methods — biometric device integration)

title: Third-party API integration for attendance
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want attendance data to be captured via API integration with third-party systems, so that attendance recorded in external tools flows into the HRMS.
acceptance_criteria: Given a third-party system is integrated via API, when it sends attendance data, then the records are received and stored against the correct employees with capture source API; Given incoming API data, when it is processed, then it is validated and made available alongside manual, biometric, and file-import records for review; Given an API payload contains malformed or unmatched records, when processing runs, then invalid records are rejected/flagged with an error reason and valid records are still ingested; Given API-sourced records, when consolidated with other sources, then duplicates are reconciled to avoid double counting
source: FR 6.18.1 (Attendance Capture Methods — API integration with third-party systems)

title: CSV/XLS attendance file import
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to import attendance from CSV/XLS files, so that I can bulk-load attendance records from external sources.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have a CSV or XLS attendance file, when I upload it, then the system parses and imports the attendance records and reports a summary of successes and failures; Given imported rows, when the import completes, then records are associated with the correct employees and dates and tagged with capture source import; Given rows with invalid employee IDs, dates, or times, when validation runs, then those rows are rejected with a clear error and valid rows are still imported; Given an imported record duplicates an existing record for the same employee and date, when consolidation runs, then duplicates are reconciled rather than double-counted
source: FR 6.18.1 (Attendance Capture Methods — CSV/XLS file import)

title: Define shift patterns and manage rosters
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to define shift patterns and manage rosters, so that employees are scheduled to the correct working shifts.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am configuring shifts, when I create a shift pattern, then I can specify its working hours, breaks, and whether it is a night shift, and reuse it across rosters; Given a roster, when I assign employees to shifts across dates, then their scheduled shifts are reflected for those dates and drive overtime and statutory evaluation; Given a roster assignment, when I edit or reassign shifts, then changes are saved and the updated schedule is visible to affected employees; Given overlapping or conflicting shift assignments, when I save the roster, then conflicts are detected and flagged
source: FR 6.18.2 (Shifts and Rosters — shift pattern definition, roster management)

title: Request a shift swap with approval
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to request a shift swap that goes through an approval workflow, so that I can exchange shifts with authorization.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am assigned a shift, when I request a swap with a colleague, then the request is routed through the defined approval workflow to the appropriate approver; Given a swap request is submitted, when it is pending, then I can view its status and the counterparty is notified; Given a swap request is approved, when the approval is recorded, then the roster is automatically updated to reflect the swapped shifts for both employees; Given a swap request is rejected, when the decision is recorded, then the original roster is unchanged and I am notified with the outcome
source: FR 6.18.2 (Shifts and Rosters — shift swapping with approval workflows)

title: Company and location-specific holiday calendars
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure company-specific and location-specific holiday calendars, so that holidays are correctly reflected for each location.
acceptance_criteria: Given I manage holiday calendars, when I add a holiday, then I can scope it to the whole company or to a specific location; Given multiple locations, when I maintain calendars, then each location can have its own distinct holiday set; Given holiday calendars are configured, when attendance is evaluated, then the applicable holidays are recognized for the relevant employees based on their location; Given a day marked as a holiday, when overtime is computed, then work on that day is eligible for holiday-category overtime
source: FR 6.18.3 (Holiday Calendars — company and location-specific configuration)

title: Optional holidays configuration
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure optional holidays in the holiday calendar, so that employees can choose from a set of optional holidays.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am configuring a holiday calendar, when I mark a holiday as optional, then it is distinguished from mandatory/fixed holidays; Given a set of optional holidays and a permitted count, when configured, then employees may select up to the allowed number of optional holidays; Given optional holidays are configured, when they are applied, then they are handled distinctly from fixed holidays in attendance and leave evaluation; Given an optional holiday is not opted by an employee, when attendance is evaluated, then that day is treated as a normal working day for that employee
source: FR 6.18.3 (Holiday Calendars — optional holidays)

title: Overtime calculation and categorization
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want overtime to be calculated and categorized as normal, holiday, or night shift, so that overtime is accurately classified for processing.
acceptance_criteria: Given attendance records, when overtime hours are computed, then they are calculated as worked hours beyond the standard scheduled/statutory hours; Given overtime is calculated, when it is stored, then it is categorized as normal, holiday, or night shift based on the day type and shift; Given work performed on a configured holiday, when overtime is categorized, then it is classified as holiday overtime; Given work performed during a night shift, when overtime is categorized, then it is classified as night-shift overtime; Given categorized overtime, when it is finalized, then it is available to payroll processing
source: FR 6.18.4 (Overtime Management — calculation and categorization)

title: Overtime eligibility and approval
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want overtime eligibility to be governed by worker category and approved through a workflow, so that only eligible, authorized overtime is finalized.
acceptance_criteria: Given a worker category, when overtime is evaluated, then only workers eligible by their category are considered for overtime and ineligible workers accrue none; Given overtime is recorded for an eligible worker, when it requires authorization, then it is routed through an approval workflow before being finalized; Given overtime is approved, when the decision is recorded, then it is marked authorized and released for payroll; Given overtime is rejected, when the decision is recorded, then it is not paid and the requester is notified with the outcome
source: FR 6.18.4 (Overtime Management — eligibility by worker category, approval workflows)

title: Attendance exception correction requests
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to raise correction requests for missed punches, late arrivals, and early exits, so that attendance errors can be corrected through an approval flow.
acceptance_criteria: Given an attendance exception such as a missed punch, late arrival, or early exit, when I submit a correction request with justification, then it is routed through the defined approval flow to the correct approver; Given a submitted request, when it is pending, then I can track its status and receive notification of the decision; Given a correction request is approved, when the decision is recorded, then the attendance record is updated accordingly; Given a correction request is not actioned within the defined threshold, when the escalation rule triggers, then the request is escalated to the next authority automatically
source: FR 6.18.5 (Attendance Exception Workflows — correction requests with approval flows and escalation rules)

title: Statutory working hours configuration
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure statutory working hours aligned with Indian labor law, so that attendance complies with legal working-hour requirements.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am configuring working hours, when I set daily working hours, weekly off, and maximum working-hour limits, then the configuration is saved and applied to the relevant employees; Given the statutory configuration, when attendance is evaluated, then it is assessed against the configured daily hours, weekly off, and maximum-hour limits; Given worked hours exceed the configured maximum daily or weekly limits, when evaluation runs, then a compliance violation is flagged for review; Given the weekly-off configuration, when attendance is evaluated, then work on weekly-off days is identified for appropriate overtime/holiday handling
source: FR 6.18.6 (Statutory Working Hours — Indian labor law alignment)

title: Administrative override with audit trail
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to override attendance records with a mandatory reason and full audit trail, so that corrections are authorized and traceable.
acceptance_criteria: Given I override an attendance record, when I save the change, then I must provide a mandatory reason before the override is accepted; Given an override is applied, when the change is persisted, then a full audit trail captures the before and after values, the reason, the acting user, and the timestamp; Given an audit trail exists, when I review override history, then all overrides for a record are visible and immutable; Given an override changes worked hours, when downstream calculations run, then overtime and statutory evaluations reflect the overridden values
source: FR 6.18.7 (Administrative Override — override with mandatory reason and full audit trail)

title: Approve or reject shift swap requests
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to review and decide on shift swap requests, so that only authorized shift exchanges are applied to the roster.
acceptance_criteria: Given a pending shift swap request, when I open it, then I can see both employees, the shifts involved, and any conflicts before deciding; Given I approve a swap, when the decision is recorded, then the roster is updated for both employees and both are notified; Given I reject a swap, when the decision is recorded, then the roster is unchanged and the requester is notified with a reason; Given a swap would create a scheduling conflict or breach statutory limits, when I review it, then the system warns me before I approve
source: FR 6.18.2 (Shifts and Rosters — shift swapping with approval workflows, approver side)

title: Approve or reject attendance correction requests and manage escalations
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to review and decide on attendance exception correction requests and handle escalations, so that legitimate corrections are applied while errors are controlled.
acceptance_criteria: Given a pending correction request for a missed punch, late arrival, or early exit, when I open it, then I can see the original record, the requested change, and the justification; Given I approve a correction, when the decision is recorded, then the attendance record is updated and the requester is notified; Given I reject a correction, when the decision is recorded, then the record is unchanged and a reason is captured; Given a request breaches the SLA threshold, when the escalation rule fires, then it is escalated to me or the next authority and appears in an escalations queue
source: FR 6.18.5 (Attendance Exception Workflows — approval and escalation, approver side)

title: Configure attendance exception approval flows and escalation rules
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure approval flows and escalation rules for attendance exceptions and overtime, so that requests are routed and escalated correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Given I configure an approval workflow, when I define approver levels and sequence, then correction and overtime requests are routed accordingly; Given I configure escalation rules, when I set time thresholds and escalation targets, then unactioned requests escalate automatically after the threshold; Given multiple approval levels, when a request is approved at one level, then it advances to the next until final authorization; Given a workflow configuration change, when saved, then it applies to subsequently submitted requests
source: FR 6.18.4, FR 6.18.5 (approval workflows and escalation rules configuration)

title: View own attendance, roster, and holidays
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to view my own attendance records, scheduled shifts, and applicable holidays, so that I can track my time and plan around holidays.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am a self-service employee, when I open my attendance, then I can view my punch history, worked hours, and any exceptions for a selected period; Given my roster is published, when I view my schedule, then I see my assigned shifts and any approved swaps; Given holiday calendars are configured for my location, when I view holidays, then I see mandatory and optional holidays applicable to me; Given optional holidays are available, when I view them, then I can opt into optional holidays up to the permitted limit
source: FR 6.18.1, 6.18.2, 6.18.3 (self-service attendance/roster/holiday visibility)

title: Manage attendance for Employee (Non-User)
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to capture and manage attendance on behalf of Employee (Non-User) records that have no self-service login, so that non-user employees are included in attendance and compliance processing.
acceptance_criteria: Given an Employee (Non-User) has no login, when their attendance is captured via manual entry, biometric, API, or file import, then it is recorded against their profile identically to user employees; Given a non-user employee has an attendance exception, when I raise a correction on their behalf, then it is processed through the approval flow with me as the requester; Given non-user attendance is recorded, when overtime and statutory evaluations run, then they are applied the same as for user employees
source: FR 6.18.1 (Attendance Capture Methods — coverage for non-user employees)

title: Attendance review dashboard and compliance reporting
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a consolidated attendance review view with compliance reporting, so that I can monitor attendance, exceptions, overtime, and statutory compliance.
acceptance_criteria: Given attendance is captured from all sources, when I open the review dashboard, then I see consolidated attendance, pending exceptions, overtime, and unmatched records for a period; Given statutory limits are configured, when I run compliance reporting, then violations of daily, weekly-off, and maximum-hour limits are highlighted; Given I need to act on issues, when I identify an exception or override need from the dashboard, then I can drill into and correct the underlying record; Given reporting is generated, when I export it, then attendance and compliance data are available for audit
source: FR 6.18.5, 6.18.6, 6.18.7 (attendance review and compliance oversight)

title: Cross-company attendance and compliance oversight
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to oversee attendance configuration and compliance across the companies in my group, so that policies are consistent and compliant group-wide.
acceptance_criteria: Given I administer multiple companies, when I review attendance settings, then I can view shift, holiday, overtime, and statutory configurations across those companies; Given company-level attendance data, when I run group-level compliance reporting, then statutory violations and overtime trends are visible across companies; Given a company deviates from policy, when I review it, then I can identify the gap and direct correction; Given data spans companies, when I access it, then I only see companies within my group scope
source: FR 6.18.3, 6.18.4, 6.18.6 (group-level attendance oversight)

title: Enable and configure attendance capture integrations
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to enable the Time and Attendance module and provision capture-method integrations, so that companies can use biometric, API, and file-import capture securely.
acceptance_criteria: Given the module is provisioned, when I enable Time and Attendance for a tenant, then its capture methods and configuration become available to company administrators; Given biometric or third-party API integrations, when I register and configure connection credentials and endpoints, then attendance data can flow in securely; Given an integration is misconfigured or credentials are invalid, when I test the connection, then a clear error is surfaced; Given file-import capability, when I enable it, then supported formats (CSV/XLS) and templates are available to admins
source: FR 6.18.1 (Attendance Capture Methods — platform-level integration enablement)

title: Bitemporal attendance data model with history and tenant isolation
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want attendance records, overrides, and corrections stored in a bitemporal, tenant-scoped data model, so that every change is historically reconstructable and isolated per tenant.
acceptance_criteria: Given attendance data is persisted, when a record is created or changed, then both the effective time (when it applied) and the transaction time (when it was recorded) are retained so prior states can be reconstructed; Given an override or correction is applied, when the new value is stored, then the previous version is preserved as history rather than overwritten; Given multiple tenants use the platform, when attendance data is stored and queried, then row-level security scopes every record to its owning company/tenant and prevents cross-tenant access; Given a point-in-time query, when I reconstruct attendance as-of a past date, then the system returns the values that were effective and known at that time
source: FR 6.18.1, FR 6.18.7 (L1 domain/data)

title: Attendance record uniqueness integrity and deduplication persistence
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want attendance records to enforce uniqueness and integrity across capture sources, so that worked hours are never double-counted and records stay consistent.
acceptance_criteria: Given records arrive from manual, biometric, API, and import sources, when they are persisted, then uniqueness constraints on employee, date, and capture context prevent conflicting duplicate entries; Given the same punch is received from more than one source, when consolidation runs, then the reconciled result is stored as the canonical record while source records remain traceable; Given a record references an employee, shift, or location, when it is saved, then referential integrity to those entities is enforced; Given punch-out precedes punch-in or overlaps another interval, when validation runs, then the record is rejected or flagged rather than persisted as valid
source: FR 6.18.1 (L1 domain/data)

title: Versioned effective-dated attendance configuration
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want shift patterns, holiday calendars, statutory rule-packs, overtime eligibility, and approval graphs managed as versioned, effective-dated configuration, so that policy changes apply from the right date without code changes.
acceptance_criteria: Given I change a shift pattern, holiday calendar, statutory limit, overtime eligibility rule, or approval/escalation graph, when I save it with an effective date, then the change applies only from that date forward and prior periods keep their previous configuration; Given governed configuration exists, when engines evaluate attendance, then they read the version of config effective for the date being evaluated; Given a configuration is superseded, when I view its history, then previous versions and their effective ranges remain visible; Given configuration is per-tenant, when I edit it, then changes affect only my company and are applied without a code release
source: FR 6.18.2, FR 6.18.3, FR 6.18.6 (L2 config/metadata)

title: Time engine computes worked hours and overtime accrual
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the shared Time/Accrual engine to derive worked hours and overtime from raw punches and schedules, so that computed time is consistent and automated.
acceptance_criteria: Given raw punch and roster data, when the Time engine runs, then it computes worked hours per employee per day against the scheduled shift and statutory standard hours; Given worked hours exceed the standard, when the engine evaluates them, then it accrues overtime hours and passes them for categorization; Given source records are later corrected or overridden, when the engine re-runs, then it recomputes worked hours and overtime deterministically from the current record state; Given night shifts, weekly-offs, and holidays, when the engine computes time, then those day/shift types are factored into the worked-hour and overtime results
source: FR 6.18.4, FR 6.18.6 (L3 engine — Accrual/Balance/Time)

title: Rules engine evaluates overtime categorization and statutory compliance
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the shared Rules engine to evaluate overtime categorization and statutory compliance via decision tables, so that classification and violation detection are config-driven, not hard-coded.
acceptance_criteria: Given decision tables for overtime categories and statutory limits, when the Rules engine evaluates a day's attendance, then it classifies overtime as normal, holiday, or night-shift per the matching rule; Given worker-category eligibility rules, when the engine evaluates a worker, then ineligible workers are excluded from overtime accrual; Given configured daily, weekly-off, and maximum-hour limits, when the engine evaluates worked hours, then breaches are flagged as compliance violations with the rule that triggered them; Given the decision tables change, when the engine next evaluates, then it applies the updated rules without code changes
source: FR 6.18.4, FR 6.18.6 (L3 engine — Rules/decision tables)

title: Workflow and notification engines route and notify on attendance requests
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want the shared workflow and notification engines to route my shift-swap, correction, and overtime requests and keep me informed, so that requests are actioned and I am notified at each step.
acceptance_criteria: Given a swap, correction, or overtime request, when it is submitted, then the Workflow engine routes it through the configured approver graph level by level until a final decision; Given a request changes state, when it is approved, rejected, or escalated, then the Notification engine sends the templated notification to the requester and relevant parties; Given an SLA threshold is reached, when the request is still unactioned, then the Workflow engine triggers escalation to the next authority automatically; Given a decision is recorded, when it is final, then the outcome is notified and the downstream record (roster or attendance) is updated
source: FR 6.18.2, FR 6.18.4, FR 6.18.5 (L3 engine — Workflow/Notification)

title: Metadata-driven attendance screens forms and search
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want metadata-driven screens, forms, and grids for attendance capture, correction, and review, so that I can interact with attendance through a consistent, configurable UI.
acceptance_criteria: Given the SPA renders from metadata, when I open an attendance capture or correction form, then its fields, validations, and labels are driven by configuration rather than hard-coded screens; Given attendance grids and dashboards, when I view records, then I can search, filter, sort, and paginate by employee, date, source, exception, and status; Given self-service, when I submit a correction or opt into optional holidays, then the form validates input client- and server-side before submission; Given theming and navigation, when I access attendance, then the screens follow the tenant theme and role-appropriate navigation
source: FR 6.18.1, FR 6.18.5 (L4 presentation)

title: Configure breaks with allowable durations
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to define named breaks with an allowable duration and applicability, so that paid and unpaid break time is governed by policy and reflected in attendance calculations.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the break configuration screen; when I add a break with a name, allowable duration in minutes, and applicability (company, location, or employee class); then the break is saved and listed for reuse; Given a break allowance is configured; when an employee's break exceeds the allowable duration; then the excess is flagged and deducted from effective worked hours; Given breaks exist; when I view the list; then each break shows its name, allowable duration, and applicability, and I can edit or refresh it
source: Kensium: Attendance Tracking > Break

title: Overtime approvers with location routing and supervisor cap
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure overtime approvers by location with a cap on the hours the immediate supervisor may approve, so that overtime is routed correctly and high-hour overtime escalates for additional authorization.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the overtime approvers screen; when I add an approver configuration specifying the applicable location(s) and approver hierarchy; then overtime requests from those locations route to the defined approvers; Given a supervisor approval cap is set; when a request exceeds the maximum overtime hours approvable by the immediate supervisor; then it requires approval from a higher authority before being finalized; Given approver configurations exist; when I edit and save them; then the updated routing applies to subsequently submitted overtime requests
source: Kensium: Attendance Tracking > Over Time Approvers

title: Raise an overtime request for extra hours worked
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to raise an overtime request for the extra hours I worked, so that my additional hours are reviewed and compensated correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Given overtime is enabled for me; when I submit an overtime request with the date and recorded overtime hours; then the request is created with status Pending and routed to my approver; Given my request is pending; when I view my requests; then I can see the overtime date, recorded hours, approved hours, and status; Given my approver decides; when the request is approved or rejected; then the approved overtime hours are recorded for payroll and I am notified of the outcome
source: Kensium: Attendance Tracking > Team Functions > Employee Over Time Request

title: Configure comp-off settings and eligibility thresholds
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to enable comp off and define settings templates with a minimum-hours threshold beyond business hours and employee-class specificity, so that compensatory off is earned only under policy.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the comp off screen; when I enable comp off and add a settings template with a name, employee-class specificity, and the minimum hours worked beyond business hours to earn comp off; then the template is saved and listed; Given a comp off template applies to an employee class; when an employee works beyond the configured threshold on an eligible day; then comp off is accrued according to that template; Given templates exist; when I view or edit them; then each shows its name, class-specific flag, minimum hours, and applicability and my changes are saved
source: Kensium: Attendance Tracking > Comp Off

title: Request compensatory off
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to request compensatory off for eligible extra hours or holiday work, so that I can take time off in lieu.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am eligible for comp off; when I submit a comp off request with the comp off date and number of days; then the request is created with status Pending and routed to my approver; Given my request is pending; when I view my requests; then I can see the comp off date, days, and status; Given my approver decides; when the request is approved or rejected; then my comp off balance is updated accordingly and I am notified
source: Kensium: Attendance Tracking > Comp Off; Team Functions > Employee Comp Off Request

title: Configure flexi schedule settings by location, department, and position
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to enable flexi schedules and scope them by location, department, or position, so that eligible employees can work flexible hours under the right rules.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the flexi schedule settings screen; when I enable flexi scheduling and choose whether it is location-, department-, or position-based; then the applicable scope options are enabled; Given a scope basis is selected; when I assign specific locations, departments, or positions to flexi scheduling; then only those groups are governed by flexi rules; Given flexi settings are saved; when attendance is evaluated for a flexi-eligible employee; then flexible start and end times are honored instead of a fixed shift
source: Kensium: Attendance Tracking > Flexi Schedule Settings

title: Configure personal out-time request limits
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure limits on personal out-time requests, so that out-time usage stays within policy.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the out time request settings screen; when I add settings defining the maximum number of requests, maximum duration per request, and maximum hours per month; then the limits are saved and enforced for employees; Given out-time settings are employee-class specific; when I set the class-specific option; then the limits apply per employee class; Given an employee attempts to exceed a configured limit; when they submit an out-time request; then the request is blocked or flagged as exceeding policy
source: Kensium: Attendance Tracking > Out Time Request Settings

title: Submit an out-time request during working hours
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to submit an out-time request for time away during working hours, so that my personal out-time is authorized and not counted against me as absence.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on my out-time request screen; when I add a request with the request type, from date-time, and to date-time; then the request is created with status Pending and the number of days and hours is computed; Given my requests exist; when I filter by period and status; then only matching requests are shown with their type, dates, duration, and status; Given my approver decides; when the request is approved or rejected; then the outcome is reflected in my attendance and I am notified
source: Kensium: Attendance Tracking > My Out Time Request

title: Configure work-from-home settings and monthly limits
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to enable work-from-home and define settings templates with a maximum number of instances per month, so that remote work is governed by policy.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the work from home settings screen; when I enable WFH and add a template with a name, employee-class specificity, and maximum WFH instances per month; then the template is saved and listed; Given a WFH template applies to an employee; when they reach the monthly instance limit; then further WFH requests are blocked or flagged; Given templates exist; when I view or edit them; then each shows its name, class-specific flag, and monthly limit and my changes are saved
source: Kensium: Attendance Tracking > Work From Home Settings

title: Submit a work-from-home request
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to submit a work-from-home request for a date range, so that my remote work is authorized and reflected in attendance.
acceptance_criteria: Given WFH is enabled for me; when I submit a WFH request with from and to date-times; then the request is created with status Pending, the number of days and hours is computed, and it is routed to my approver; Given my request is within the monthly limit; when it is submitted; then it is accepted for approval, otherwise it is rejected as over-limit; Given my approver decides; when the request is approved or rejected; then my attendance for those days reflects the outcome and I am notified
source: Kensium: Attendance Tracking > Team Functions > Employee Work From Home Request

title: Configure work-from-home approvers
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure the approvers for work-from-home requests, so that WFH requests are routed to the correct authorities.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the work from home approvers screen; when I add an approver configuration with the applicable scope and approver hierarchy; then WFH requests route to those approvers; Given multiple approval levels are configured; when a request is approved at one level; then it advances to the next until final authorization; Given approver configurations exist; when I edit and save them; then the updated routing applies to subsequently submitted WFH requests
source: Kensium: Attendance Tracking > Work From Home Approvers

title: Register biometric tracking devices at entry and exit points
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to register attendance tracking devices at entry and exit points with their location and alert email, so that punches are captured accurately and device issues are notified.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the tracking device screen; when I add a device with a name, office location, work area, entry/exit type, tracking mode, and alert email; then the device is saved and listed; Given a device is registered; when it captures a punch; then the record is attributed to the device's location and entry/exit type; Given a device fails or reports an issue; when an alert condition occurs; then a notification is sent to the configured alert email
source: Kensium: Attendance Tracking > Tracking Device

title: Configure tracking modes, reconciliation, and reminders
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure attendance tracking modes and how records from multiple devices are reconciled, so that attendance from different capture methods is consolidated correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the tracking mode screen; when I add a tracking mode (such as self-service portal, access-control reader, or manual) with a description and applicable locations; then the mode is saved and listed; Given multiple tracking modes capture for the same employee; when I choose reconciliation by priority or grace period and set the grace period and minimum break duration; then attendance is consolidated using that basis; Given employees have not recorded attendance; when reminders are enabled by Email or SMS; then reminders are sent to those employees before the payroll cutoff
source: Kensium: Attendance Tracking > Tracking Mode

title: View my shift assignments
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to view my shift assignments with their schedule and approval status, so that I know which shifts I am scheduled to work.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on my shift assignment screen; when the list loads; then I see each assignment's shift name, from and end date, start and end time, assigned-by, assigned date, and status; Given I want a specific period or shift; when I filter by date range, shift, or status; then only matching assignments are shown; Given an assignment's status is Pending approval, Approved, Rejected, or Cancelled; when I view it; then its current status is clearly displayed
source: Kensium: Attendance Tracking > My Shift Assignment

title: Configure attendance change-request approvers with payroll-aligned second level
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to configure attendance change-request approvers and require a second-level approval for requests raised after the payroll date, so that late corrections receive additional oversight.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the change request approvers screen; when I add approvers with their applicability by location, department, and position; then change requests route to the correct approvers; Given I set the payroll period date of every month; when a change request is raised after that date; then a configured second-level approval is enforced before the correction is applied; Given approver configurations exist; when I search by location, department, or position; then only matching configurations are shown
source: Kensium: Attendance Tracking > Change Request Approvers

title: Review and approve pending attendance change requests
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to review pending attendance change requests and approve or reject them, so that corrected attendance is applied only after validation.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the pending change request screen; when I filter by period, status, employee class, department, or position; then only matching requests are listed, including those pending with me; Given a change request exists; when I open it; then I can compare the original in and out times with the requested in and out times, tracking mode, and duration; Given a request is pending; when I approve or reject it via the task action; then the attendance record is updated to reflect the decision and the requester is notified
source: Kensium: Attendance Tracking > Team Functions > Pending Change Request

title: Mass-approve team attendance for a date
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to review and approve attendance for many employees on a given date at once, so that I can process daily attendance efficiently before payroll.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the attendance mass approval screen; when I pick a date and optionally filter by department; then the grid loads attendance for the matching employees on that date; Given the grid is loaded; when records are displayed; then I see each employee's planned work, actual start and end, work location, number of breaks, break duration, overall duration, and effective hours; Given I have reviewed the records; when I approve one or more rows via the task action; then their attendance status is updated in bulk
source: Kensium: Attendance Tracking > Team Functions > Attendance Mass Approval

title: Record a bulk manual attendance sheet scoped by location, department, and position
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to record manual attendance for multiple employees in one sheet scoped by location, department, and position, so that I can efficiently capture attendance with exact punches and comments.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the manual attendance sheet screen; when I choose applicable locations, departments, and positions and select employees; then the sheet is scoped to those employees; Given the sheet is scoped and an attendance date is chosen; when I add in-time and out-time rows with in-time and out-time comments; then each punch row is added to the grid; Given I have entered the punches; when I submit the sheet; then the manual attendance is saved for all selected employees and the date, and each record is traceable to me as the entering user
source: Kensium: Attendance Tracking > Team Functions > Manual Attendance Sheet

title: Schedule recurring attendance audits
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to define recurrence patterns for attendance audits by location and work area, so that attendance audits run automatically on a defined schedule.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the audit recurrence pattern screen; when I add a recurrence pattern with its schedule, location, and work area; then the pattern is saved and listed; Given a recurrence pattern exists; when the scheduled time is reached; then the attendance audit for that location and work area runs automatically; Given patterns exist; when I view or edit them; then each shows its recurrence, location, and work area and my changes are saved
source: Kensium: Attendance Tracking > Audit Recurrence Pattern

title: View my attendance breakdown with breaks and effective duration
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to view my daily attendance with the breakdown of breaks, effective duration, and work-location categories, so that I understand exactly how my working hours are allocated.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on my attendance details screen; when the list loads; then each day shows planned work, actual start and end time, at-work time, break count and break duration, effective duration, and status; Given I want a specific period or today; when I filter by a from and to date range or select today's data; then only the matching records are displayed; Given my time is split across categories; when I view a record; then I can see official visit, client location, personal off, and work-from-home portions of my time
source: Kensium: Attendance Tracking > My Attendance Details
